jokes.text: Tony claims he drove Neil Armstrong and when asked where he took him, says 'The unemployment office'
jokes.setup: Tony excitedly tells everyone he drove the famous astronaut Neil Armstrong
jokes.punchline: The hero who walked on the moon is now unemployed and can't pay cab fare
jokes.explanation: Strong subversion of expectations with economic delivery. Takes American hero worship and deflates it perfectly through economic reality.

jokes.text: Tony shows off what he claims is a piece of the moon that Neil Armstrong gave him instead of cab fare
jokes.setup: Tony says Armstrong gave him something more valuable than money
jokes.punchline: It's allegedly a moon rock from a bag Armstrong had
jokes.explanation: Perfect for Tony's gullible character - he'd absolutely believe this obvious scam. Good character integration.

jokes.text: Bobby suggests the Neil Armstrong might be a phony, and Tony responds 'if he was a phony, where did he get a piece of the moon?'
jokes.setup: Bobby questions whether the Neil Armstrong was real
jokes.punchline: Tony's circular logic uses the fake evidence to prove the fake person
jokes.explanation: Brilliant circular logic that perfectly captures Tony's naive worldview. The logic is so backwards it's genius.

jokes.text: Louie says McKenzie 'had my butt for breakfast'
jokes.setup: Louie is explaining he's in trouble with the boss
jokes.punchline: Crude but vivid metaphor for being chewed out
jokes.explanation: Typical Louie crude expression. Functional but not particularly original.

jokes.text: Louie promises not to fire whoever slept in the office, just 'briefly torture you with a soldering iron'
jokes.setup: Louie promises he won't make a big deal about someone sleeping in the office
jokes.punchline: His idea of 'not a big deal' is torture with hot metal
jokes.explanation: Perfect Louie - sadistic but absurd. The specificity of 'soldering iron' makes it funnier than generic torture threats.

jokes.text: Everyone realizes Latka is in the bathroom and calls his name
jokes.setup: Louie is demanding to know who slept in the office
jokes.punchline: The obvious suspect is literally in the bathroom right now
jokes.explanation: Simple reveal comedy. The timing works but it's fairly predictable.

jokes.text: Latka!
jokes.setup: Everyone's looking around wondering who slept in the office
jokes.punchline: Louie immediately identifies the culprit
jokes.explanation: Simple reveal moment. Gets the job done but nothing special.

jokes.text: Latka responds to being called with 'Is occupied' from the bathroom
jokes.setup: Everyone calling Latka's name urgently
jokes.punchline: He misunderstands and thinks they're checking if the bathroom is free
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Latka's innocent misunderstanding of the situation's urgency.

jokes.text: Louie yells 'Get that thing out of your mouth!' at Latka who is brushing his teeth
jokes.setup: Latka emerges from bathroom clearly having been living there
jokes.punchline: Louie's outrage at basic hygiene activities
jokes.explanation: Louie's misplaced outrage at normal activities is funny. Visual element adds to the absurdity.

jokes.text: Louie forces Latka to swallow toothpaste, and Latka says 'Mmm! Not bad.'
jokes.setup: Louie demands Latka swallow his toothpaste
jokes.punchline: Latka actually enjoys the taste
jokes.explanation: Latka's innocent reaction to Louie's cruelty. Good physical comedy with unexpected positive response.

jokes.text: When told the cleaning lady found 'a strange man' sleeping in the office, Latka asks 'How did she know I was strange?'
jokes.setup: Louie describes the cleaning lady finding a 'strange man'
jokes.punchline: Latka focuses on being called strange rather than being caught
jokes.explanation: Perfect Latka logic - completely missing the point while revealing self-awareness. Excellent misdirection.

jokes.text: Louie tells Latka 'You can work here, you can sweat here, you can even die here, but you cannot live here.'
jokes.setup: Louie explaining garage rules to Latka
jokes.punchline: Death is acceptable but sleeping is not
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation showing workplace priorities. Dark humor with great rhythm and quotable structure.

jokes.text: Someone tells Louie to 'take a nice, deep breath and never let it out'
jokes.setup: Suggesting Louie calm down with breathing exercises
jokes.punchline: Actually suggesting he suffocate himself
jokes.explanation: Clever disguised insult. Sounds like helpful advice until you think about it.

jokes.text: When asked why his building was torn down, Latka says 'Because it would not burn.'
jokes.setup: Question about why Latka's building was demolished
jokes.punchline: Implies arson was the preferred method but the building was too sturdy
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark and economical. Reveals both terrible living conditions and casual acceptance of insurance fraud.

jokes.text: When told to go to his ambassador for help, Latka reveals 'But ambassador was my roommate.'
jokes.setup: Suggestion that Latka get help from his country's embassy
jokes.punchline: The ambassador was also homeless and living in the condemned building
jokes.explanation: Fantastic escalation that makes the situation impossibly worse while being perfectly logical for Latka's mysterious country.

jokes.text: Alex describes an apartment offer as 'One room for one night including roommate and complimentary cocktail'
jokes.setup: Alex explaining the apartment hunting results
jokes.punchline: Clearly describing a prostitution setup disguised as housing
jokes.explanation: Good euphemistic description of a sketchy situation. Dark humor about housing desperation.

jokes.text: Louie says he threw Latka's stuff in the street himself after Latka thought a 'terrible person' did it
jokes.setup: Latka complains about someone throwing his belongings out
jokes.punchline: Louie proudly admits to being the 'terrible person'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Louie moment - takes pride in being cruel. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: Latka says the apartment manager wants to 'put me on a leash' instead of 'lease'
jokes.setup: Latka describing apartment rental terms
jokes.punchline: Mispronunciation creates image of being treated like a dog
jokes.explanation: Typical Latka malapropism that works for his character. The misunderstanding creates a funny mental image.

jokes.text: When asked about taking the bus, Latka says 'it's parked on my sport coat'
jokes.setup: Discussion about how to get to the apartment
jokes.punchline: Latka's belongings are scattered with a bus literally on his jacket
jokes.explanation: Absurd visual situation delivered matter-of-factly. Good callback to his belongings being thrown out.

jokes.text: Alex repeatedly saying 'Latka!' in increasing disbelief at the luxury apartment
jokes.setup: Alex seeing the expensive apartment Latka has rented
jokes.punchline: Mounting horror expressed through repetitive name-calling
jokes.explanation: Good escalating reaction beat. Shows Alex's growing panic through repetition.

jokes.text: Latka asks 'Why do you keep saying my name over and over?'
jokes.setup: Alex repeatedly saying Latka's name in shock
jokes.punchline: Latka genuinely doesn't understand the reaction
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Latka's obliviousness to the magnitude of his mistake.

jokes.text: Learning the apartment costs $3,000 a month when Latka only has $3,000 total
jokes.setup: Latka proudly explaining his new expensive apartment
jokes.punchline: He can afford exactly one month and doesn't understand recurring payments
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup for the episode's central premise. Latka's innocent misunderstanding of basic economics is hilarious.

jokes.text: Oh, what am I going to do next month?
jokes.setup: Learning that $3000/month means every month, not just once
jokes.punchline: Latka's innocent question about future payment
jokes.explanation: Perfect naivety from Latka. The innocent delivery makes the financial reality even funnier.

jokes.text: When the maid appears, Latka says 'See? It comes with a maid.'
jokes.setup: Latka trying to justify the apartment's value
jokes.punchline: He thinks the maid is included like an appliance
jokes.explanation: Shows Latka's innocent misunderstanding of wealth and service. Good character moment.

jokes.text: Alex comparing the apartment residents to 'the Rockefellers, the Vanderbilts, the Bee Gees'
jokes.setup: Listing wealthy people who live in such places
jokes.punchline: Including the Bee Gees as equivalent to industrial dynasties
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with unexpected pop culture reference that dates the show. Works for the period.

jokes.text: Latka saying goodbye to luxury items: 'Good-bye, couch. Good-bye, carpet. Good-bye, lush contemporary decor.'
jokes.setup: Latka being forced to give up the apartment
jokes.punchline: Treating inanimate objects like beloved friends
jokes.explanation: Sweet and pathetic moment showing Latka's childlike attachment. Creates sympathy while being funny.

jokes.text: Well, I just wanted to say that, uh, we-we'll take it.
jokes.setup: Alex calling to cancel Latka's lease after the goodbye speech
jokes.punchline: Instead accepts the apartment
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection after building up the departure. Sets up the apartment storyline continuing.

jokes.text: So, are you ready for nik-nik?
jokes.setup: Maid asking if Latka wants something
jokes.punchline: Her euphemistic offer that clearly means sex
jokes.explanation: Sexual innuendo that's obvious but works for the luxury apartment setting. Functional comedy.

jokes.text: Uh, yes, my swizzle stick is a little limp.
jokes.setup: Maid asking if Alex needs anything
jokes.punchline: Alex's mock-sophisticated complaint with sexual undertones
jokes.explanation: Alex playing up the rich guy role with double entendre. Good character moment showing his adaptation.

jokes.text: Tony suggests to avoid embarrassment of formal announcement: 'Don't leave'
jokes.setup: Complaint about formal announcement every time someone leaves
jokes.punchline: Tony's simple solution is to never leave
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tony logic - simple solution to complex etiquette problem. Good character integration.

jokes.text: Tony ordering increasingly elaborate drinks from the maid: foreign beer, frosted mug, make it two, peanuts
jokes.setup: Tony saying he hates to be waited on
jokes.punchline: Immediately exploiting the service with growing demands
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing Tony's immediate corruption by luxury. Perfect character hypocrisy.

jokes.text: Bobby's over-the-top self-introduction: 'A gentleman who needs no introduction... an artist in a world of philistines... our last hope for the life of the theater... a man who bestrides this world like a colossus... ladies and gentlemen, the legend we call Robert Wheeler.'
jokes.setup: Bobby making yet another grand entrance
jokes.punchline: Increasingly grandiose and pretentious self-description
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bobby - pretentious actor completely absorbed in his own importance. Long but worth it for character.

jokes.text: Alex points out 'that's your 14th grand entrance of the day'
jokes.setup: Bobby's dramatic entrance
jokes.punchline: Alex has been counting them all day
jokes.explanation: Great callback that shows Alex's exasperation. The specific number makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Tony and Bobby both want to bring dates and flip a coin, with Tony calling heads and Bobby saying 'I win. I get the game room.'
jokes.setup: Coin flip to determine who gets which room for dates
jokes.punchline: Bobby declares victory regardless of the actual result
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bobby's selfishness and Tony's naivety in accepting it.

jokes.text: Tony's response to getting the master bedroom: 'I guess I'll just have to figure out something to do in the master bedroom.'
jokes.setup: Tony 'losing' the coin flip and getting the bedroom
jokes.punchline: Pretending the bedroom is a consolation prize
jokes.explanation: Tony's fake disappointment at getting the better room. Good character comedy.

jokes.text: Elaine in the Jacuzzi: 'Oh, I love every wrinkle on every toe.'
jokes.setup: Elaine enjoying the luxury
jokes.punchline: Overly detailed appreciation of her own pruney feet
jokes.explanation: Mild physical comedy about pruny skin. Shows luxury making people appreciate small things.

jokes.text: Latka explains sharing in his country: 'You know, in my country, everyone shares with everyone... Otherwise they shoot you.'
jokes.setup: Romantic notion about communal sharing
jokes.punchline: Reveals it's enforced by violence
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark comedy subversion. Starts as idealistic philosophy, ends as totalitarian threat. Brilliantly constructed.

jokes.text: Elaine's relaxation is interrupted by 'There's a Mr. Louie De Palma here to see you' and she responds 'I just remembered' (tension and unhappiness)
jokes.setup: Elaine saying she can't remember tension and unhappiness
jokes.punchline: Louie's arrival instantly reminds her
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing and setup/payoff. Louie's effect on everyone is perfectly encapsulated.

jokes.text: Bobby trying to have Louie turned away: 'I don't recall a De Palma. Send him packing.'
jokes.setup: Bobby trying to act like mansion owner
jokes.punchline: Pretending not to know his own boss
jokes.explanation: Good Bobby character moment - putting on airs and trying to exclude Louie.

jokes.text: Louie's request to the maid: 'Inga... suds.'
jokes.setup: Louie encountering the maid service
jokes.punchline: Crude, minimal request for beer
jokes.explanation: Perfect Louie economy - reduces elegant service to caveman grunt. Very quotable.

jokes.text: Louie's compliment: 'This is a nice apartment. You're going to feel like killing yourselves when you got to leave.'
jokes.setup: Louie giving what seems like a compliment
jokes.punchline: Immediately follows with crushing psychological prediction
jokes.explanation: Classic Louie - can't give a compliment without adding poison. Perfect character integration and dark humor.

jokes.text: This is a nice apartment. You're going to feel like killing yourselves when you got to leave.
jokes.setup: Louie seemingly giving a compliment
jokes.punchline: Immediately following with psychological torture
jokes.explanation: Quintessential Louie - can't give a compliment without twisting the knife. Perfect character integration and cruel wisdom.

jokes.text: Response to Louie being locked out: 'Quick, get some boards and nails' / 'Are you kidding? Our booze is in there.'
jokes.setup: Suggestion to board up the room to keep Louie out
jokes.punchline: Practical concern overrides the desire to exclude him
jokes.explanation: Good practical comedy - hatred of Louie vs. need for alcohol. Realistic priority setting.

jokes.text: Louie announces 'By the way... you're out of scotch' after being locked out
jokes.setup: Louie being excluded from the party
jokes.punchline: Reveals he drank their alcohol while locked out
jokes.explanation: Perfect Louie vindictiveness. Great callback and escalation of his destructiveness.

jokes.text: Elaine's reason for staying another month: 'Do you know that right now my kids are down at the swimming pool taking swimming lessons, and in the afternoon, they have tennis lessons, and the elevator operator is teaching them French.'
jokes.setup: Discussion of staying longer in the apartment
jokes.punchline: Increasingly elaborate services that somehow include the elevator operator as French tutor
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of luxury services. The elevator operator detail is wonderfully absurd.

jokes.text: Tony's promise about the rent party: 'I ain't making no promises, but maybe I can get Neil Armstrong here.'
jokes.setup: Discussion of getting people for the rent party
jokes.punchline: Tony still believes his fake Neil Armstrong is real and available
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback showing Tony's continued gullibility. Good character consistency across episode.

jokes.text: At the failed party, someone says 'some of the women around here are real dogs' and a dog barks in response
jokes.setup: Complaint about unattractive women at the party
jokes.punchline: Actual dog takes offense at the comparison
jokes.explanation: Simple visual pun. Works as a quick gag but not particularly sophisticated.

jokes.text: Alex's speech about not needing women: explains how they didn't need women as kids, then ends with 'I'm going to try to get to know each and every one of you here tonight... then I'm gonna go out and chase down some chicks, of course.'
jokes.setup: Elaborate speech about male bonding and not needing women
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicts entire speech with admission he'll chase women
jokes.explanation: Great long setup with perfect deflation. Shows everyone's real motivation despite noble speeches.

jokes.text: When Elaine offers to dance, the men surge forward and Tony says 'Just step back a little. Give her some room... Elaine will dance, we will watch.'
jokes.setup: Elaine trying to help the failed party by dancing
jokes.punchline: Tony organizing the men like they're watching entertainment
jokes.explanation: Cringey moment showing how desperate and inappropriate the situation has become.

jokes.text: Tony talking to imaginary person named Don: 'Don, see Elaine dance? Yeah. Dance, Elaine.'
jokes.setup: Tony organizing the entertainment
jokes.punchline: Talking to someone who apparently doesn't exist
jokes.explanation: Absurd moment suggesting party is so bad Tony is hallucinating guests.

jokes.text: Alex tells Latka 'No one with a friend like you can be called a loser.'
jokes.setup: Latka feeling bad about losing the apartment
jokes.punchline: Compliment that actually insults everyone else by implication
jokes.explanation: Sweet moment with subtle self-deprecating humor. Good character development for Alex.

jokes.text: Latka's reflection: 'You know, for me to live in a place like this has been a miracle. And someday, when things are bad, and I have no money and no food, and I'm depressed, I will think about this month that I lived here, and I will feel even worse.'
jokes.setup: Latka reflecting on the positive experience
jokes.punchline: Realizes the memory will make future poverty more painful
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy that starts sweet and becomes devastating. Perfect Latka logic and beautifully crafted reversal.

jokes.text: Latka's final interaction with the maid: 'Nik-nik?' 'Oh, thank you very much.'
jokes.setup: Latka alone in the apartment on his last night
jokes.punchline: Final acceptance of the mysterious service he kept refusing
jokes.explanation: Sweet ending to running gag. Shows character growth and acceptance of luxury before losing it.
